lesson.menu_desc: Capstone -- put it all together: wishes, regrets, conditionals and linkers to tell your own English journey -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Telling Your Story -- Putting It All Together
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sandra Hayasaki | Aula 20 -- Telling Your Story

listenings.text: Let me tell you the story of my own language journey. The beginning was not easy. Years ago, I could only say a few words, and I was afraid to speak. If I had given up then, I would not be here today. But I kept going. The turning point came when I read my grandmother's old letters. That memory still makes me grateful. Although progress was slow, every small milestone felt huge. I remember the first time I told a long story without stopping. I wish I had started earlier, but I do not waste time on regret. When I recall those years, I feel proud. The lesson of my journey is simple. Your story is never finished. There is always a new chapter, a new word, a new beginning. So keep telling your story, one sentence at a time.

I want to tell you my own English story so far. The beginning feels far away now. At first, I only knew a handful of words, and I was a little shy. If someone had told me I would speak this much, I would not have believed them. The turning point was the day I decided that age was not a wall. I am fifty-one, and I am proud of every milestone. I recall my first full conversation, and I still feel grateful for it. Although the road was long, I never gave up. I wish I had more free time to study, but I make the most of every lesson. When I look back, I have very few regrets. Each new chapter of my journey opens a door. This is not the end. It is one more beginning, and I am ready for the next part of my story.

extra_audio.text: First, set the beginning of your story, for example when I started, I knew very little. Then, describe the turning point, the moment that changed something, like the day I decided to keep going. Next, add a wish or a small regret, such as I wish I had started earlier. After that, name a milestone you are proud of, for example my first full conversation. Finally, close with hope, like this is just one more chapter, and I am ready for the next.
